title: Schoenus bifidus
taxonomy: Schoenus bifidus is classified as a species within the genus Schoenus, which belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The family Cyperaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sedges or cattails. The genus Schoenus is a group of plants that are typically found in wetland or aquatic environments. The classification of Schoenus bifidus reflects its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Cyperaceae family. Schoenus bifidus is also known as the forked sedge, a name that reflects its distinctive appearance.

intro: Schoenus bifidus, also known as the forked sedge, is a plant species within the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is native to Australia. The forked sedge has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schoenus bifidus include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the forked sedge is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, although its use in modern herbal medicine is less well-documented.
